tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000 square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

tool_args.label: Climate Resiliency Work at Bronx-Whitestone and Verrazzano-Narrows Bridges
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Construction & Development is nearing substantial completion on a design-build contract for power redundancy and resiliency improvements at the Bronx-Whitestone and Verrazzano-Narrows bridges. The $80 million contract, awarded in December 2022, is expected to be substantially completed by the end of this year.

payload.project_name: Climate Resiliency Work at Bronx-Whitestone and Verrazzano-Narrows Bridges
payload.description: Power redundancy and resiliency improvements at the Bronx-Whitestone and Verrazzano-Narrows bridges. Includes installation of a second 5 kV cable, second set of electrical switchgear, new emergency generator, replacement of obsolete SCADA system, flood wall installation, roof replacement, and replacement of obsolete mechanical equipment. At Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge: upgraded electrical substations, replaced SCADA system, replaced lane control signals and support equipment.

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA Construction & Development is nearing substantial completion on a design-build contract for power redundancy and resiliency improvements at the Bronx-Whitestone and Verrazzano-Narrows bridges. The $80 million contract, awarded in December 2022, is expected to be substantially completed by the end of this year.
The Bronx-Whitestone Bridge service building and lower garage are situated in a flood-prone neighborhood of the Bronx...

installation of a second 5 kV cable across the bridge and a second set of electrical switchgear. A new emergency generator was installed to provide tertiary backup for facility power. The obsolete supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) system was replaced
installation of a flood wall around the service building to protect against future flood events... replacement of the roof and obsolete mechanical equipment, such as boilers and HVAC units

The contract includes similar work at the Verrazzano-Narrows Bridge, where existing electrical substations have been upgraded; the obsolete SCADA system has been replaced and incorporated into the Authority-wide traffic management system; and existing lane control signals and support equipment have been replaced.
